advantages: Reveals non-linear local structure and curved manifolds that linear PCA projections often miss.
Produces intuitive 2D/3D maps for EDA on embeddings, genomics, images, and high-dimensional tabular data.
Perplexity gives a tunable knob for neighborhood scale; heavy-tailed Student-t kernel addresses the crowding problem.
Widely available in sklearn and standard ML stacks with Barnes–Hut acceleration for moderate n.
disadvantages: Does not preserve global distances—cluster size, inter-cluster gaps, and axis positions are often misleading.
O(n²) exact cost and stochastic optimization make it slow and non-deterministic compared to PCA.
Standard t-SNE does not embed new points without refitting or specialized extensions.
Hyperparameter-sensitive (perplexity, learning rate, preprocessing); unsuitable as a default feature-reduction step before supervised training.
